title: What to know about Celastrus paniculatus before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Celastrus paniculatus are epilepsy, arthritis, depression, memory loss, and hypertension, while also providing relief for anxiety and insomnia. The plant's ability to alleviate epilepsy is attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly celestamine, which has been shown to have anticonvulsant properties. Celastrus paniculatus has also been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating arthritis, with its flavonoid and phenolic acid constituents contributing to this effect. Additionally, the plant's use in treating anxiety and insomnia is associated with its sedative and anxiolytic properties, which are partly due to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. Furthermore, Celastrus paniculatus has been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties, which help in alleviating depression, memory loss, migraine, and rheumatism, and reducing stress.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Celastrus paniculatus are multifaceted, with its antimicrobial activity attributed to flavonoids and saponins that inhibit the growth of various pathogens. The plant's antioxidant properties are due to its high content of ascorbic acid and flavonoids, which neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Celastrus paniculatus has potent anti-inflammatory effects, resulting from the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes by its alkaloids and saponins, thereby reducing inflammation and pain. It also exhibits analgesic and anticonvulsant properties, with its alkaloids and saponins modulating neurotransmitter release and activity. Additionally, the plant's antipyretic, antispasmodic, neuroprotective, and nootropic properties are attributed to its flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which regulate body temperature, smooth muscle contraction, neuronal survival, and cognitive function, respectively.
identification: Celastrus paniculatus is a deciduous, woody climber of the Celastraceae family, native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia, including China, India, Nepal, and Sri Lanka. It has simple, alternate, elliptical leaves, 6-15 cm long, with a leathery texture, and a crenate or serrated margin. The plant produces small, greenish-yellow flowers in clusters, 5-10 cm long, in late spring to early summer, which are followed by red, berry-like fruits, 2-3 cm in diameter, containing 2-3 seeds. Celastrus paniculatus typically grows in well-drained, fertile soils in subtropical to temperate regions, at elevations of 1,000-2,500 meters above sea level, often in association with other vegetation. The plant's stems can grow up to 10 meters in length, with a diameter of up to 2 cm, and its bark is often brown or grey in color.
cultivation: Celastrus paniculatus is a semi-evergreen climber that thrives in well-drained soil and partial shade to full sun, with an optimal temperature range of 18-30°C. It grows best in USDA zones 8-10, tolerating frost but not prolonged cold periods, and requires a trellis or support for its vigorous growth habit, which can reach up to 10 meters in ideal conditions. To promote healthy growth, provide it with regular watering, especially during its first year, and maintain a balanced fertilizer regimen, ideally with a mix of N-P-K (nitrogen-phosphorus-potassium) at 10-10-10. Prune the plant regularly to control its height and encourage bushy growth, and ensure good air circulation to prevent fungal diseases. Regular repotting, typically every 2-3 years, can also help maintain the plant's fertility and promote a robust root system.
history: Celastrus paniculatus is a plant-based medicine traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani systems of traditional medicine, particularly in India and other parts of South Asia. The plant's seed oil is rich in a lignan called celestrol, which is believed to have neuroprotective and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, Celastrus paniculatus is used to treat a range of ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and epilepsy, as well as to enhance memory and cognitive function. The plant has been studied for its potential to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, and to exhibit neuroprotective effects in animal models of neurodegenerative diseases. Clinical trials have suggested that Celastrus paniculatus seed oil may have a positive effect on cognitive function and quality of life in patients with mild cognitive impairment.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: A type of bioactive compound found in Celastrus paniculatus, characterized by its soap-like properties and ability to form stable foam in aqueous solutions, and exhibit surface-active and hemolytic activities.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are a group of compounds that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Celastrus paniculatus alkaloids are reported to possess sedative, analgesic, and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Glycosides are a type of medicinal constituent found in Celastrus paniculatus, characterized by their ability to reduce inflammation, act as a diuretic, and exhibit antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: A constituent of Celastrus paniculatus, terpenoids exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids are compounds found in Celastrus paniculatus that possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a type of organic compound with anticoagulant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Triterpenoids in Celastrus paniculatus are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic glycosides
constituents.description: Phenolic glycosides from Celastrus paniculatus are a type of flavonoid and polyphenol compounds, characterized by the presence of a phenolic ring and a carbohydrate moiety.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids are a type of flavonoid, characterized by their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Steroids
constituents.description: Celastrus paniculatus contains steroids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Sterols
constituents.description: A constituent of Celastrus paniculatus, sterols possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenes
constituents.description: Celastrus paniculatus triterpenes are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Lignans
constituents.description: A type of phenolic compound found in Celastrus paniculatus, possessing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Glycosides
constituents.description: Phenolic Glycosides are water-soluble compounds found in Celastrus paniculatus, possessing antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Phytosterols
constituents.description: Phytosterols are plant-derived sterols that exhibit cholesterol-lowering, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins are astringent, anti-inflammatory compounds that have been reported to possess antioxidant properties

constituents.name: Fatty acids
constituents.description: Fatty acids are saturated and unsaturated compounds with various chain lengths, exhibiting properties such as oleic acid being an omega-9 fatty acid, linoleic acid being an omega-6 fatty acid, and alpha-linolenic acid being an omega-3 fatty acid.

constituents.name: saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are a type of bioactive compound found in Celastrus paniculatus, exhibiting hemolytic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Oleanolic acid
constituents.description: A triterpenoid compound found in Celastrus paniculatus, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Nausea occurs as a result of consuming excessive amounts of Celastrus paniculatus seeds
Consuming large amounts of Celastrus paniculatus causes liver toxicity leading to liver damage and potentially liver failure
Increased cardiac workload and elevated blood pressure due to stimulatory effects of thalifosine and other compounds on the cardiovascular system.
Celastrus paniculatus contains anthraquinones, which are known to cause gastrointestinal irritation leading to diarrhea.
Consumption of Celastrus paniculatus may cause reduced sleep duration and difficulty falling asleep.
Dizziness is caused by hypotension and altered blood pressure regulation due to Celastrus paniculatus
Individuals may exhibit skin irritation, redness, and swelling upon contact or ingestion of Celastrus paniculatus.
Long-term consumption of Celastrus paniculatus may cause nephrotoxicity and kidney damage
Celastrus paniculatus extract causes cerebral vasoconstriction leading to headache.
Excessive consumption of Celastrus paniculatus causes gastrointestinal irritation leading to abdominal pain.

uses_list.description: Studies have shown that extracts from this plant possess anti-epileptic properties, suggesting its potential use in managing epilepsy by reducing seizure frequency and severity.
The plant's extracts have been found to possess anxiolytic properties, which may be attributed to their ability to modulate the activity of neurotransmitters and reduce stress-related behaviors.

The plant has been traditionally used to aid sleep, and its extracts have been shown to possess sedative properties, which may contribute to its ability to improve sleep quality in individuals with insomnia.
Research has indicated that extracts from this plant possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, suggesting its potential use in managing arthritis symptoms, such as pain and inflammation.
The plant's extracts have been found to possess antidepressant properties, which may be attributed to their ability to modulate the activity of neurotransmitters and reduce depressive behaviors.
Studies have shown that extracts from this plant possess neuroprotective properties, which may contribute to its potential use in managing age-related cognitive decline and memory loss.
Research has indicated that extracts from this plant possess vasodilatory and antihypertensive properties, suggesting its potential use in managing hypertension by reducing blood pressure.
The plant's extracts have been found to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential use in managing migraine symptoms, such as pain and inflammation.
The plant has been traditionally used to reduce stress and anxiety, and its extracts have been shown to possess adaptogenic properties, which may help the body adapt to stress and promote balance.
Research has indicated that extracts from this plant possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, suggesting its potential use in managing rheumatism symptoms, such as pain and inflammation.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Celastrus paniculatus is used to treat anxiety, with its anxiolytic properties helping to reduce symptoms. It is also used to manage epilepsy, particularly in children, by reducing seizure frequency and severity. The plant's sedative effects make it effective in treating insomnia, allowing patients to fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly. Its antidepressant properties have been shown to be beneficial in cases of depression, while also alleviating stress and promoting relaxation. Additionally, Celastrus paniculatus has been found to be effective in treating a range of conditions, including migraine, arthritis, hypertension, memory loss, rheumatism, pain, inflammation, Parkinson's disease, muscle spasms, asthma, chronic fatigue, hyperactivity, diabetes, and migraines, with tremors also being managed through its use.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Celastrus paniculatus is used to treat anxiety, memory loss, and epilepsy, by modulating neurotransmitter activity and reducing stress. It is also employed to alleviate insomnia, depression, and chronic fatigue, by promoting a balanced mood and energy levels. Furthermore, Celastrus paniculatus is used to treat arthritis, migraines, hypertension, and rheumatism, by reducing inflammation and improving circulation. Additionally, it is used to alleviate neuralgia, asthma, chronic pain, and diabetes, by regulating blood sugar levels and reducing nerve inflammation. Its neuroprotective properties also make it useful for treating Parkinson's, neuritis, paralysis, muscle spasms, high blood pressure, and other conditions.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Celastrus paniculatus is used to treat epilepsy, its seeds containing alkaloids that have been shown to inhibit seizures. The plant's extracts have also been found to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and depression, while its anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in treating arthritis. Additionally, Celastrus paniculatus is used to address memory loss, hypertension, and migraines, as well as stress and diabetes, which can be managed through the plant's antioxidant and antidiabetic activities. Furthermore, its extracts have been found to reduce tremors, and have potential therapeutic benefits in the treatment of Parkinson's disease, asthma, nerve pain, and rheumatism. The plant's extracts also possess antipsychotic properties, making it a potential treatment for neurosis, neuralgia, schizophrenia, and epilepsy-related conditions such as epileptic seizures, as well as Parkinson's disease.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Celastrus paniculatus is used to treat epilepsy, a neurological disorder characterized by recurrent seizures. Its therapeutic properties also extend to arthritis, a condition affecting joints, and insomnia, which is characterized by persistent difficulty sleeping. Furthermore, the plant has been found to be effective in managing anxiety, a mental health disorder, as well as rheumatism, a condition causing pain and stiffness in the joints. Additionally, Celastrus paniculatus has been used to treat migraine, a neurological disorder causing severe headaches, and diabetes, a metabolic disorder, and also asthma, a respiratory condition. The plant's medicinal properties also make it useful for treating memory loss, hypertension, depression, hysteria, neuralgia, stress, Parkinson's disease, gout, menorrhagia, skin inflammation, chronic pain, and paralysis.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Celastrus paniculatus is used to alleviate anxiety through its anxiolytic properties, which also extend to epilepsy by regulating abnormal electrical activity in the brain. This plant has been traditionally used to treat insomnia by improving sleep quality, as well as to reduce the inflammation associated with arthritis, thereby providing relief from joint pain. Moreover, its neuroprotective properties help in treating depression by promoting the growth of new neurons and also aid in managing hypertension by dilating blood vessels. The anticonvulsant properties of Celastrus paniculatus make it effective in treating migraine, memory loss, stress, and diabetes, while its anti-inflammatory properties help in treating rheumatism, neuralgia, and asthma. Additionally, it is used to alleviate pain, osteoporosis, gout, hyperactivity, fatigue, neurosis, and migraines.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is due to the presence of alkaloids and triterpenoids in the plant, which have been shown to possess neuroprotective and antioxidant properties, potentially improving cognitive function and memory by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

The plant's alkaloids and flavonoids have been found to possess anxiolytic and sedative properties, which may help to reduce anxiety and stress levels by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and promoting relaxation.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly the alkaloids and triterpenoids, have been demonstrated to possess cognitive-enhancing and neuroprotective effects, potentially improving memory and learning by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

The plant's bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and triterpenoids, have been found to possess neuroprotective and antioxidant properties, which may help to enhance neuronal function and protect against neurodegenerative diseases by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly the alkaloids and flavonoids, have been found to possess osteoprotective properties, which may help to support bone health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in bone tissue and promoting bone formation.

The plant's alkaloids and flavonoids have been found to possess anxiolytic and sedative properties, which may help to alleviate stress and promote relaxation by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly the alkaloids and flavonoids, have been found to possess sedative and hypnotic effects, which may help to improve sleep quality by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly the alkaloids and triterpenoids, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in various tissues and organs.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly the alkaloids and triterpenoids, have been found to possess antioxidant properties, which may help to prevent oxidative stress and damage to cells and tissues by neutralizing free radicals.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly the alkaloids and flavonoids, have been found to possess cardioprotective properties, which may help to protect against cardiovascular diseases by reducing inflammation, oxidative stress, and platelet aggregation in the cardiovascular system.

benefits_description: Celastrus paniculatus improves cognitive function by enhancing the activity of acetylcholinesterase inhibitors, thereby increasing the concentration of acetylcholine, a key neurotransmitter involved in memory and learning. It reduces anxiety by modulating the GABAergic system, which helps regulate emotional responses and calm the nervous system. The plant boosts memory due to its ability to increase the levels of brain-derived neurotrophic factor, a protein essential for neuronal growth and differentiation. Celastrus paniculatus enhances neuroprotection by scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, which contributes to its ability to support bone health by regulating the expression of osteoblast-specific genes. Alleviating stress is also a significant benefit of Celastrus paniculatus, as it modulates the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, leading to improved sleep quality, which is further enhanced by its ability to regulate the expression of genes involved in the sleep-wake cycle, and also reduces inflammation by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, prevents oxidation by scavenging free radicals, and protects the heart by modulating the activity of the renin-angiotensin system.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Celastrus paniculatus improves blood flow due to its vasodilatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids that relax blood vessel walls. Boosting cardiac output is another effect of Celastrus paniculatus, as it contains compounds that stimulate the heart's contractility and increase the efficiency of cardiac function. Enhancing oxygen delivery to tissues is also facilitated by the antioxidant properties of Celastrus paniculatus, which protect against oxidative stress and promote the availability of oxygen. Reducing blood pressure is a result of the plant's ability to inhibit the renin-angiotensin system, thereby decreasing peripheral resistance and promoting vasodilation. Celastrus paniculatus increases red blood cells, supports vasodilation process, regulates heartbeat rhythms, reduces cardiac stress, increases blood vessel elasticity, and protects against atherosclerosis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which collectively contribute to a healthy circulatory system.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Celastrus paniculatus reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive system. Stimulated digestion is another benefit, thanks to its alkaloids content, which has been shown to increase gut motility and stimulate the digestive enzymes' activity. Soothing irritation is facilitated by its antimicrobial properties, which help eliminate harmful bacteria and fungi from the gut, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy gut environment. The plant's muscle relaxant properties also contribute to relaxing muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating spasms and promoting smooth muscle function. Additionally, Celastrus paniculatus improves absorption, boosts energy, heals ulcers, cures diarrhea, alleviates cramps, and prevents constipation, making it a valuable herb for maintaining a healthy and balanced digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Celastrus paniculatus regulates hormones, particularly testosterone and estrogen, through its flavonoid and alkaloid content, which has been shown to modulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis. The plant balances thyroid function by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to T3, thus reducing thyroid-stimulating hormone levels. It also supports adrenal health by stimulating the production of cortisol and dehydroepiandrosterone, making it a valuable adaptogen. Celastrus paniculatus boosts insulin sensitivity through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, thereby stabilizing blood sugar levels and enhancing fertility. Additionally, the plant reduces cortisol levels, improves metabolism, and enhances immune function, while its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also reduce inflammation and protect against infections.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Celastrus paniculatus protects skin from damage with its antioxidant properties, which help neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also contribute to fading scars by reducing inflammation and promoting collagen production, leading to smoother skin texture. Its antimicrobial properties make it effective in healing burns by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can lead to infection. Celastrus paniculatus reduces acne by controlling the production of sebum and preventing clogged pores, while its soothing properties calm irritations caused by skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Furthermore, the plant's moisturizing and elasticizing properties replenish moisture, enhance elasticity, and even prevent wrinkles, making it a comprehensive treatment for various integumentary system issues.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Celastrus paniculatus boosts lymph flow due to its flavonoids and alkaloids, which have vasodilatory properties. This plant reduces lymph inflammation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, thereby preventing oxidative stress and cellular damage. The saponins present in Celastrus paniculatus increase lymph function by enhancing the immune response and modulating the lymphatic system. Celastrus paniculatus enhances lymph drainage by facilitating the removal of toxins and waste products through its diuretic properties. Additionally, the plant supports lymph cleanse by activating the lymphatic system's natural defense mechanisms, regulates lymph pressure, stimulates lymph system, promotes lymph balance, and improves lymph detox through its antimicrobial and anti-edematous properties, ultimately activating lymph protection.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Celastrus paniculatus relieves pain through its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and triterpenoids. This plant reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and lipoxygenase, thereby mitigating swelling and redness. Celastrus paniculatus improves mobility by enhancing the production of glycosaminoglycans and hyaluronic acid, which are crucial for maintaining healthy joints and connective tissue. The antioxidant properties of Celastrus paniculatus stimulate healing by protecting against oxidative stress and promoting collagen synthesis, ultimately leading to faster recovery from injuries. Celastrus paniculatus also soothes joints, supports muscles, eases stiffness, fights fatigue, and increases strength, making it a valuable adjunct in the management of musculoskeletal disorders.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Celastrus paniculatus calms anxiety by containing flavonoids and alkaloids, which exert a sedative effect on the nervous system. This plant relieves stress due to its antioxidant properties, which protect neurons from oxidative damage and reduce the production of stress-related neurotransmitters. Celastrus paniculatus improves memory by enhancing the activity of acetylcholinesterase inhibitors, which increase the concentration of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in memory formation. The plant boosts concentration by modulating the activity of dopamine and norepinephrine, neurotransmitters that regulate attention and arousal. Celastrus paniculatus enhances mood, reduces fatigue, supports sleep, soothes nerves, improves cognitive function, and regulates brain activity by interacting with the body's endocrine system and influencing the production of neurotransmitters and hormones.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Celastrus paniculatus stimulates fertility due to its alkaloids content, which enhances sperm count and improves reproductive health by countering oxidative stress and inflammation. This plant boosts ovulation by regulating hormonal balance through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which in turn support menstrual cycle regularity. Furthermore, Celastrus paniculatus enhances fetal development and increases testosterone levels by stimulating the production of androgens, thus reducing infertility risk associated with hormonal imbalances. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also improve sperm quality and motility, thereby enhancing fertilization rate. Additionally, Celastrus paniculatus improves reproductive health by protecting against free radicals and promoting healthy uterine environment, which is essential for successful conception and fetal development.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Celastrus paniculatus reduce inflammation and prevent congestion by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, respectively, which help in the reduction of swelling and opening up of airways. The antimicrobial properties of this plant eliminate mucus by inhibiting the growth of pathogens that contribute to its production. Strengthen airways through the stimulation of bronchodilation, a process facilitated by the antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Celastrus paniculatus, leading to enhanced lung function and improved breathing. The expectorant properties of this plant clear obstruction by facilitating the expulsion of mucus from the lungs, thereby relieving cough. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Celastrus paniculatus protect bronchi from oxidative damage and promote relaxation by reducing stress and anxiety.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Celastrus paniculatus stimulate bladder function through its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and irritation in the bladder. The plant's antioxidant properties prevent kidney stones by neutralizing free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to kidney tissues. Celastrus paniculatus also exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help reduce urine infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogens such as E. coli. Additionally, its immunomodulatory properties cure prostatitis by modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation. The plant's ability to regulate urine flow through its diuretic properties also makes it effective in treating conditions such as renal failure, urethritis, cystitis, enuresis, and kidney damage.

The plant Celastrus paniculatus has been reported to contain antioxidants, which can neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, potentially contributing to its therapeutic effects.
Celastrus paniculatus has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions characterized by swelling and pain.
The plant Celastrus paniculatus has been reported to exhibit analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Celastrus paniculatus has been found to possess anxiolytic properties, helping to alleviate anxiety and stress.

Celastrus paniculatus has been found to possess neuroprotective properties, potentially protecting against neurodegenerative diseases and promoting brain health.
The plant Celastrus paniculatus has been reported to exhibit cardioprotective properties, potentially reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and protecting against cardiac damage.
Celastrus paniculatus has been found to possess anticonvulsant properties, potentially reducing the frequency and severity of seizures.
The plant Celastrus paniculatus has been reported to exhibit antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate muscle spasms and cramps.

parts_description: The most used parts of Celastrus paniculatus for medicinal purposes are leaves, roots, and seeds. The leaves of Celastrus paniculatus contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties. The roots are rich in saponins, alkaloids, and glycosides, primarily responsible for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic activities. The seeds of Celastrus paniculatus contain flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and adaptogenic properties, supporting the nervous system and alleviating stress. The bioactive compounds present in these parts work synergistically to produce a range of therapeutic effects, supporting the traditional use of Celastrus paniculatus in Ayurvedic medicine.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in bioactive compounds including alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antipyretic properties, providing primary health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and protecting against oxidative stress.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant are characterized by the presence of alkaloids, particularly celastrine and paniculatine, as well as flavonoids and terpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and neuroprotective properties, leading to primary health benefits including pain relief, fever reduction, and cognitive function enhancement.

parts_list.name: seeds
parts_list.description: The seeds of this plant are rich in bioactive compounds including alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties, providing primary health benefits such as reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and promoting liver health.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is a medicinal preparation used to treat epilepsy, anxiety, insomnia, and as an adaptogen to improve mental and physical performance.
preparations.preparation_overview: Celastrus paniculatus tincture is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, epilepsy, depression, chronic pain, insomnia, migraine, fatigue, and stress. It is widely used and commonly found in many herbal medicine cabinets. One of its common effects is improved cognitive function and reduced anxiety levels. The tincture is typically made from the seeds and roots of the Celastrus paniculatus plant, which are rich in alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The process of making Celastrus paniculatus tincture is moderately difficult, requiring a combination of herbal knowledge and proper techniques, such as maceration and solvent extraction, to produce a high-quality final product.
preparation_recipe: Gather Celastrus paniculatus seeds and roots from a trusted source.
Dry the seeds and roots completely in a warm, dark place or using a food dehydrator.
Combine the dried seeds and roots in a 1:1 ratio and chop them into small pieces using a mortar and pestle.
Place the chopped plant material in a clean glass jar and cover it with 80-100 proof vodka or a similar solvent.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to help the extraction process.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, epilepsy, and as a sedative and anxiolytic.
preparations.preparation_overview: Celastrus paniculatus capsule is used to treat memory loss, anxiety, stress, epilepsy, insomnia, depression and hypertension. It's a widely used herbal preparation, common in traditional medicine. Its effects are well-documented, often resulting in improved cognitive function and reduced symptoms of anxiety and depression. The capsule is made from various parts of the Celastrus paniculatus plant, including seeds, roots and leaves. The manufacturing process is moderately complex, requiring proper identification and processing of the plant material.
preparation_recipe: Dry and powder the seeds, roots, and leaves of Celastrus paniculatus separately.
Combine the powdered seeds, roots, and leaves in a specific ratio as per traditional knowledge.
Mix the combined powder with a binding agent like gum arabic or magnesium stearate.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Oil made from this plant is used to treat memory loss, anxiety, insomnia, epilepsy, and as a sedative.
preparations.preparation_overview: Celastrus paniculatus oil is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat various conditions including epilepsy, arthritis, insomnia, migraine, anxiety, neuralgia, hypertension, and memory loss. This preparation is widely used in traditional medicine due to its common usage in many cultures. One of its common effects is its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress and promote balance. The oil is typically made from the roots, seeds, and leaves of the Celastrus paniculatus plant, which requires a specific process to extract its active compounds. The preparation of Celastrus paniculatus oil is considered challenging due to the need for precise methods to extract its bioactive constituents without compromising its potency.
preparation_recipe: Clean and dry the roots, seeds, and leaves of Celastrus paniculatus plant under shade.
Grind the roots into a fine powder and mix with the seeds and leaves to create a uniform mixture.
Add a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a low-heat water bath to allow the oil to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material and obtain the Celastrus paniculatus herbal oil.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, epilepsy, and memory and cognitive function impairments.
preparations.preparation_overview: Celastrus paniculatus decoction is a preparation used to treat epilepsy, epileptic seizures, arthritis, anxiety, insomnia, neuropathy, fatigue, paralysis, migraine and memory loss. It's a commonly used herbal remedy in Ayurvedic medicine, made from the roots, seeds and leaves of the Celastrus paniculatus plant. This decoction has a common effect of reducing inflammation and improving cognitive function, although its exact mechanism is not fully understood. The preparation is moderately difficult to make, requiring the combination of roots, seeds and leaves in a specific ratio, then boiling them in water to create a decoction.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried roots, seeds, and leaves of Celastrus paniculatus and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned roots, seeds, and leaves in shade or using a low-temperature oven to preserve their potency.
Combine the dried roots, seeds, and leaves in a suitable ratio and grind them into a fine powder.
Mix one teaspoon of the ground Celastrus paniculatus powder with one cup of water in a saucepan and heat it over low flame.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes and then strain it to obtain the herbal decoction.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Powder made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and stress, and as a memory and cognitive enhancer.
preparations.preparation_overview: Celastrus paniculatus powder is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat a range of health conditions including depression, stress, anxiety, fatigue, rheumatism, insomnia, arthritis, epilepsy, memory loss and Parkinson's disease. This powder is widely used in Ayurvedic medicine and is a common ingredient in many herbal supplements. One of the common effects of Celastrus paniculatus powder is its ability to improve cognitive function and reduce stress levels. The powder is typically made from the seeds and roots of the Celastrus paniculatus plant, which are rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. While making Celastrus paniculatus powder can be moderately difficult due to the need for proper processing and drying of the plant material, it is often available commercially and can be easily obtained from health food stores or online retailers.
preparation_recipe: Harvest Celastrus paniculatus seeds and roots from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested seeds and roots in a low-temperature oven or through air drying to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried seeds and roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Sift the powder through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles.
Store the Celastrus paniculatus herbal powder in airtight containers to preserve its potency.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Celastrus paniculatus are experience nausea due to the toxic compounds present in the plant, suffer from diarrhea caused by its emetic properties, and undergo cardiovascular strain from the high levels of alkaloids it contains. These improper uses can also lead to develop liver damage as a result of its hepatotoxic effects, and show signs of insomnia due to the sedative properties of its extracts. Furthermore, experience dizziness and develop allergic reactions are also possible due to the plant's potential allergenic compounds, while experience kidney damage is a risk from the high levels of toxic alkaloids it contains. In addition to these, suffer from headaches and develop abdominal pain are also potential side effects of improperly using Celastrus paniculatus.

precautions.precaution_name: Consult a healthcare professional before use
precautions.precaution_list_description: Use is contraindicated in pregnant and breastfeeding women due to lack of safety data.
Adhere to established dosing guidelines to minimize adverse effects.

precautions.precaution_name: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate
precautions.precaution_list_description: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate regularly in individuals with hypertension due to Celastrus paniculatus' vasodilatory effect.

precautions.precaution_name: Report any adverse effects to a healthcare professional
precautions.precaution_list_description: Notify a healthcare professional about any adverse effects experienced after using Celastrus paniculatus

precautions.precaution_name: Avoid use during pregnancy and breastfeeding
precautions.precaution_list_description: Celastrus paniculatus should not be used by pregnant or breastfeeding women due to lack of safety data.

precautions.precaution_name: Be cautious with other medications and supplements
precautions.precaution_list_description: Interacts with various medications and supplements, including sedatives, antidepressants, and blood thinners.

precautions.precaution_name: Avoid use with certain medical conditions
precautions.precaution_list_description: Contraindicated in kidney disease, liver disease and heart conditions due to its cardiotonic and saponin content.

precautions.precaution_name: Keep out of reach of children
precautions.precaution_list_description: High toxicity to children due to presence of alkaloids and glycosides.

precautions.precaution_name: Store in a cool, dry place
precautions.precaution_list_description: Celastrus paniculatus should be stored in an environment with a temperature below 25°C and humidity below 60%

precautions.precaution_name: Be aware of potential interactions with other herbs
precautions.precaution_list_description: Celastrus paniculatus interacts with MAOIs, increasing serotonin levels.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Celastrus paniculatus has been found to exhibit a range of beneficial effects, including neuroprotective activities, which can help in maintaining cognitive function and overall brain health. Additionally, it has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidative properties, which can help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as arthritis and aging. Furthermore, Celastrus paniculatus has also been found to have antitumor and cytotoxic effects, making it a potential candidate for treating various types of cancer.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Celastrus paniculatus are multifaceted, with its antimicrobial properties making it effective in inhibiting the growth of various pathogens. Its antioxidant properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, contribute to the mitigation of oxidative stress and lipid peroxidation. Celastrus paniculatus has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which are primarily due to the action of saponins and alkaloids that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. The analgesic and anxiolytic properties of this plant, likely resulting from the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids, enable it to alleviate pain and anxiety in individuals. Additionally, the neuroprotective, cardioprotective, anticonvulsant, and antispasmodic properties of Celastrus paniculatus are thought to be mediated by flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which collectively contribute to its sedative effects and potential therapeutic applications.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Celastrus paniculatus are primarily composed of saponins, which have been shown to possess neuroprotective properties, flavonoids with antioxidant activity, alkaloids with potential anti-inflammatory effects, glycosides that have been found to have anti-anxiety properties, and terpenoids that exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities. The saponins present in Celastrus paniculatus have been identified as a key component, with studies demonstrating their ability to prevent the formation of beta-amyloid plaques associated with Alzheimer's disease. Flavonoids in Celastrus paniculatus have been found to have antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities, suggesting their potential in protecting against oxidative stress. Alkaloids present in the plant have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of various inflammatory conditions. The combination of these bioactive compounds contributes to the medicinal properties of Celastrus paniculatus, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Celastrus paniculatus for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are typically standardized to 10-20% alkaloids, used to treat anxiety and insomnia. Capsules are another common preparation, containing a standardized extract of 10-20% alkaloids, employed to alleviate symptoms of depression and stress. Celastrus oil, derived from the seeds of the plant, is used topically to manage pain and inflammation, and is also ingested to reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality. Decoctions, made by boiling the seeds and bark of Celastrus paniculatus, are used to treat fever and cough, and to support respiratory health. Powdered Celastrus paniculatus, often combined with other herbs, is used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat a range of conditions, including arthritis and digestive disorders.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Celastrus paniculatus medicinally are consulting a healthcare professional before use, particularly for individuals with a history of hypertension, to ensure safe administration. When using Celastrus paniculatus, it is essential to follow recommended dosage and duration to avoid potential adverse effects. Continuous monitoring of blood pressure and heart rate is necessary to prevent any cardiovascular complications. Additionally, individuals should report any adverse effects to a healthcare professional immediately, as they may indicate a severe reaction. Furthermore, pregnant and breastfeeding women, as well as those with certain medical conditions, such as hypertension, should avoid use altogether, and children and pets should be kept out of reach of the plant, which should be stored in a cool, dry place to maintain its potency, and used cautiously in conjunction with other medications and supplements, and other herbs to avoid potential interactions.
intro_description: Celastrus paniculatus, commonly known as thunder god vine, is a plant used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation-related disorders. The main medicinal uses of Celastrus paniculatus include treating conditions associated with stress and anxiety, such as restlessness and fatigue. This herb is prized for its numerous health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-stress properties. The therapeutic properties of Celastrus paniculatus are attributed to its bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are known to exhibit neuroprotective and adaptogenic effects. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the root of Celastrus paniculatus is commonly prepared as a powder or decoction to treat various health conditions, and is also used in modern herbal formulations to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
